description:
GOAL: Replace thread-local runtime state (HEAP, MAP_BUILDER, JIT_CTX,
others) with an explicit `ctx: *mut Process` first argument threaded
through every FFI fn. Sets up multi-thread scheduling (a near-term goal
per user, 2026-05-11), closes the re-entrance footgun, and makes
thread-safety obvious from FFI signatures rather than implicit in TLS.

## Decisions captured (user, 2026-05-11)

- De-TLS strategy: OPTION 3a — pass `*mut Process` as a hidden first
  argument to every runtime FFI fn. NOT option 3c (TLS-held ptr).
  Reasoning: multi-thread is near-term; explicit ctx in signatures
  (i) makes per-process scope visible at every call site, (ii) closes
  the re-entrance hole where TLS could go stale if a future FFI fn runs
  user code that yields, (iii) enables `send(receiver_pid, msg)` to
  pass both sender_ctx and receiver_ctx explicitly without back-channel,
  (iv) is faster than TLS access on hot path (extra register-passed arg
  is free on modern ABIs; TLS access is `__tls_get_addr` indirect call).

## What lives on Process (defined here; .19.1 builds the Process struct)

For this ticket, treat Process as a struct with at minimum:

```rust
pub struct Process {
    pub heap: Heap,                  // was thread_local! HEAP
    pub map_builder: MapBuilder,     // was thread_local! MAP_BUILDER
    pub jit_ctx: Option<JitCtx>,     // was thread_local! JIT_CTX
    // .19.1 adds: pid, next_frame, mailbox, state
}
```

This ticket creates the struct as a stepping-stone; fz-ul4.19.1
attaches scheduler-level fields. If .19.1 lands first, this ticket
slots fields into the existing Process struct.

## FFI signature migration

Every runtime fn in src/ir_codegen.rs currently shaped like:

```rust
extern "C" fn fz_alloc_struct(schema_id: u32) -> *mut u8 { ... }
```

becomes:

```rust
extern "C" fn fz_alloc_struct(ctx: *mut Process, schema_id: u32) -> *mut u8 {
    let process = unsafe { &mut *ctx };
    process.heap.alloc_struct(schema_id)
}
```

Concrete site list (audit src/ir_codegen.rs; this list is initial, expand
during implementation):

- fz_alloc_frame (line ~1415)
- fz_alloc_struct (line ~444)
- fz_alloc_list_cons (line ~428)
- fz_alloc_bitstring (line ~722)
- fz_alloc_map (line ~540)
- fz_alloc_closure (line ~1329)
- fz_alloc_float (line ~924, ~973, ~996)
- fz_map_push / fz_map_finish (use MAP_BUILDER, line ~460+)
- fz_print_value (line ~139)
- All other `HEAP.with(...)` call sites (greppable: ~20 sites)

Each gets a ctx prepended.

## Cranelift emitter changes (src/ir_codegen.rs)

At every emit site that lowers a Prim/Term to a Call instruction:

- Signature builder: prepend `AbiParam::new(types::I64)` (ctx ptr).
- Call site: load ctx from a known register/local. The trampoline (.97-127)
  receives ctx from outside (the scheduler hands it in); the trampoline
  passes ctx as the first arg to every dispatched fn. Each JIT'd fn keeps
  ctx in a known frame slot or threads it through its own internal calls.

Decide during implementation: keep ctx in a Cranelift block-param threaded
through every block, OR store it in the frame at a fixed offset (e.g.
frame[24]) on entry and reload as needed. Block-param is simpler; frame-
slot saves reg pressure for fns with many internal calls. RECOMMENDED:
block-param for v1; frame-slot is a perf follow-up.

## Trampoline change (src/ir_codegen.rs:97-127)

```rust
pub fn run(process: &mut Process, entry_frame: *mut u8) -> FzValue {
    let mut cur = entry_frame;
    let ctx = process as *mut Process;
    loop {
        if cur.is_null() { break; }
        // (.11.31 safepoint check fires here)
        let schema_id = read_schema_id(cur);
        let fn_ptr = process.fn_table[schema_id];
        cur = unsafe { fn_ptr(ctx, cur) };   // ctx first arg
    }
    extract_result(cur)
}
```

The trampoline becomes per-Process. The scheduler (.19.1) drives multiple
Processes by calling `process.step()` rather than `process.run()`-to-
completion.

## Removing TLS

After migration, the following thread_local!s in ir_codegen.rs (and
elsewhere) are deleted:

- HEAP (line ~395)
- MAP_BUILDER (line ~460)
- (audit; possibly VEC_BUILDER, BS_BUILDER if they exist)
- JIT_CTX (jit.rs:175)

TLS for test-only state (TEST_CAPTURE, IR_TEXT_RECORD) STAYS — those are
test scaffolding, not runtime state. Document why each remaining TLS is
allowed.

## What to leave shared (NOT on Process)

- Atom table (fz_runtime::intern / name_of): explicitly shared, per
  fz-ul4.19.6 decision (global singleton for v1).
- Schema registry: currently per-Heap, but really shared structurally
  (schemas describe code, not per-process state). For v1, leave the
  registry inside Heap (it gets cloned/shared via Rc); revisit if it
  becomes a hot path.
- Code memory / Cranelift module: shared across processes. Fns are
  immutable code; only frame data is per-process.

## Tests

- All existing tests pass after migration (this is largely mechanical).
- A test that constructs two `Process` instances on the same OS thread,
  runs each through a small program independently, asserts no
  cross-contamination: e.g., proc A builds map {a: 1, b: 2}, proc B
  builds map {c: 3}, both finish correctly. (Pre-migration, this would
  fail because MAP_BUILDER is shared TLS.)
- Verify FFI signature: codegen unit test asserts every emitted Call has
  the ctx arg in slot 0.

## Risks

- Mechanical hazard: large diff touching every FFI site + every emit
  site. Mitigation: do it in one go, run full test suite after.
- Breaking the trampoline ABI mid-migration. Mitigation: land in a single
  commit; no half-migrated state.
- Performance regression: extra arg might increase register pressure on
  small fns. Mitigation: benchmark count(100000, 0) before/after; expect
  noise-level difference on modern ABIs.

## Out of scope

- Scheduler / spawn / pid (.19.1, .19.2).
- Multi-thread scheduler synchronization (atomics on Heap flags, atom
  table locking, etc.) — separate concern; this ticket only enables it.
- Splitting Heap into per-arena (.19.5).

## References

- src/ir_codegen.rs:395-401 (HEAP TLS)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:460-462 (MAP_BUILDER TLS)
- src/jit.rs:175 (JIT_CTX TLS)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:97-127 (trampoline — gains ctx arg)
- src/heap.rs:73-96 (Heap struct — becomes Process.heap)

## Acceptance

- All TLS runtime state is gone from ir_codegen.rs and jit.rs (test TLS
  excepted, with comment).
- Trampoline takes `&mut Process`.
- Two-Process test passes.
- One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.32.
title: Per-Process runtime context: thread ctx ptr through FFI (de-TLS)
